metadata.title: States PCS: Assam GK Questions for Assam Public Service Commission Exams

questions.question: Who ruled Kamarupa from approx. 374 to 398 AD, consolidating Varman dynasty?
questions.explanation: Balavarman I ruled Kamarupa from 374 to 398 AD and consolidated the Varman dynasty’s rule. The Varman dynasty was the first historical dynasty of Kamarupa in ancient Assam. Balavarman I held the title Maharajadhiraja during his reign, establishing strong centralized control over the region.

questions.question: Which religion was dominant among ancient Kamarupa’s ruling dynasties?
questions.explanation: Ancient Kamarupa’s rulers primarily practiced Saivism. The Kalika Purana mentions sects like Kalikas and Pasupatas during that period.

questions.question: What was the term for revenue-free land grants to Brahmanas in ancient Assam?
questions.explanation: Brahmadaya were revenue-free land grants specifically given to Brahmanas in ancient Assam. Devottara and Dharmottara lands were assigned to temples or religious institutions. Simapradatta referred to land grants that marked territorial boundaries in the region.

questions.question: According to Kalika Purana, which temple was central to ancient Kamarupa?
questions.explanation: Kalika Purana identifies Kamakhya Temple as the religious center of the ancient kingdom of Kamarupa, located in present-day Assam. The temple is dedicated to the goddess Kamakhya and is a prominent Shakti Peeth. The Kalika Purana is a Hindu scripture composed in the first millennium CE, focused on the goddess and rituals associated with her worship.

questions.question: Swarnapitha, an ancient Kamarupa Pitha, lies between which Assam rivers?
questions.explanation: Swarnapitha is one of the four ancient Kamarupa Pithas located between the Rupali and Bharali rivers in Assam. Kamarupa was an early historical state in Assam, and its division into pithas is based on river boundaries. The Rupali and Bharali rivers define the geographical extent of Swarnapitha.

questions.question: Who founded the Ahom Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Chaolung Sukapha founded the Ahom Kingdom in 1228. The Ahom Kingdom was located in present-day Assam. It lasted for nearly 600 years. King Naranarayan ruled the Koch dynasty in Assam later.

questions.question: Which 18th-century chronicle documents Ahom-Kachari kingdom relations?
questions.explanation: Kachari Buranji is a historical chronicle that records relations between the Ahom and Kachari kingdoms from the 14th to 18th centuries. It is an important Assamese historical document. The Padshah Buranji relates mainly to Ahom kings’ narratives. Jaintia and Tripura Buranjis cover history relevant to different kingdoms in Northeast India. Kachari Buranji specifically covers Kachari-Ahom interactions.

questions.question: Who was the early Kamata ruler who shifted the capital to Kamatapur and took the title Kamateswara?
questions.explanation: Sandhya was a Kamata ruler who shifted the capital to Kamatapur after repelling Malik Ikhtiyaruddin Iuzbak’s 1257 attack. He adopted the title Kamateswara. This event took place in the 13th century during the Kamata kingdom’s history in Assam.

questions.question: Which Ahom king defeated the Chutias in 1552, expanding north of Tiphao river?
questions.explanation: In 1552, Ahom king Suhungmung defeated the Chutias and expanded the kingdom north of the Tiphao river. Suhungmung pushed the boundaries of the Ahom kingdom during his reign, consolidating power in the region.

questions.question: What was the official title of the Ahom dynasty kings?
questions.explanation: The Ahom kings were officially titled Swargadeo, meaning “Lord of the Heavens.” The Ahom dynasty ruled Assam from 1228 to 1826, establishing the Swargadeo as the supreme authority.
